text.title: Solo Context Switch Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when applying solo-context-switch-protocol. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Day starts with Twitter/Slack/email; by noon the founder feels behind and has shipped nothing.
antipattern.root-cause: Inbox-first wrecks the morning attentional window; the 23-min recovery cost compounds.
antipattern.fix: Enforce Builder block first, before any inbox glance. Phone in another room or DND for the Builder window.

antipattern.symptom: Operator block re-loads mental state from scratch; "what was I doing in Builder?" eats 15min per transition.
antipattern.root-cause: Block transitions without the 5-min handoff note force the brain to rebuild context.
antipattern.fix: Mandate the 3-question handoff note (what was open / next decision / next mode must remember). 5 min cost, 15 min saved.

antipattern.symptom: Every customer DM marked "urgent"; Builder block fragmented; daily violations >3 for 3 consecutive weeks.
antipattern.root-cause: The override list expanded informally; nothing fits the original P0/charge-dispute/security definition.
antipattern.fix: Re-read the closed override list; if items don't fit, write them to next Operator block; structural fix (lengthen Operator block, hire support) if violations persist.
